# pack_trust test fixtures (Phase 14)

Signed-pack fixtures for the Phase 14 chain-of-trust tests. The fixture
JSON files themselves are GENERATED by `forge-sign` in plan 14-03 (the
signer must exist before real signed fixtures can) — this directory is
seeded in plan 14-01 so every downstream plan references a stable path.

## Planned fixtures

| File | Contents | Exercises |
|------|----------|-----------|
| `valid-signed.json` | Pack body + signature block produced by `forge-sign sign` with a root-issued cert | TRUST-01 happy path (import succeeds) |
| `tampered-body.json` | `valid-signed.json` with one body field edited AFTER signing (title changed) | TRUST-03 `TamperedPack` rejection, zero DB writes |
| `stripped-signature.json` | `valid-signed.json` with the top-level `signature` block removed, `licensed:` provenance retained | D-09 `MissingSignature` rejection for licensed/curated tiers |
| `forged-cert.json` | Pack signed by a key whose issuer cert is NOT signed by the bundled root (self-signed cert) | `UntrustedIssuer` rejection (chain step 1 fails) |

## Conventions

- All identifier/hash/signature-adjacent values are JSON **strings**, never
  numbers (JCS float-precision rule — 14-RESEARCH Pitfall 3, resolved Q1).
- Signature block shape (D-04, minimal): `{alg, issuerCert, keyFingerprint, sig}`
  with `issuerCert = {issuerId, name, publicKeyPem, rootSig}` (D-05).
- Fixtures are consumed by `learnforge-core` unit tests and the
  `src-tauri` import-gate integration tests (plan 14-04).
